subject: Anthropology
title: Warfare in Small Societies
description: A 60-second lesson on Warfare in Small Societies.

hook.text: Yanomami raids kill one or two people. Medieval battles killed thousands. Scale matters when studying warfare.

buildup.text: Warfare in small-scale societies involves raids, ambushes, and skirmishes between kin groups or neighboring communities.

discovery.text: Causes range from revenge and resource competition to prestige, bride capture, and violations of sacred boundaries.

twist.text: Small-scale war is often ritualized. Battles may end after the first casualty. Total war is a modern state invention.

climax.text: War is cultural, not instinctual. How, when, and why people fight is shaped by social rules, not just aggression.

quiz.question: How does warfare in small societies differ from state warfare?
options: It's often ritualized and may end after the first casualty
